item.title: 44-year-old Ticinese convicted for Lugano cellar thefts
item.description: A 44-year-old Ticinese man has been convicted of stealing from Lugano cellars to fund his cocaine addiction
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- 44-year-old Ticinese man convicted for burglaries in Lugano cellars
- Two years and two months in prison for funding cocaine addiction
- Traded stolen goods with drug dealers

## Key facts
- **What**: Burglaries in private cellars
- When: For over a year and a half, from March 2022 to October 2023
- Where: Municipalities of Lugano, Carona, Collina d'Oro, and Grancia
- Who: 44-year-old Ticinese resident of Lugano
- Amount: Two years and two months in prison, fine of 5,000 Swiss francs

A 44-year-old Ticinese man has been convicted for a series of burglaries in private cellars in the Lugano area. The man, who acted for over a year and a half, was sentenced to two years and two months in prison and a fine of 5,000 Swiss francs. According to the investigations, the burglaries were aimed at financing his cocaine addiction. Furthermore, the defendant traded part of the stolen goods with drug dealers to obtain the drug.

### Security implications

This case raises important questions about the security of private cellars in the region. Many residents may now consider installing alarm systems or surveillance cameras to protect their belongings. Additionally, the case highlights the link between minor crimes and addictions, a problem that requires an integrated approach from the authorities.

The cellars in the Lugano area are often used for storing fine wines and other valuable items. The conviction of the 44-year-old Ticinese serves as a warning to other potential criminals and as a reminder for residents to adopt adequate security measures.

### Concrete examples and real numbers

During the period of the burglaries, the defendant stole goods valued at over 50,000 Swiss francs. Among the stolen items were bottles of fine wine, sports equipment, and valuable objects. The victims of the burglaries were residents of the municipalities of Lugano, Carona, Collina d'Oro, and Grancia.

### Regulations and laws

According to Article 139 of the Swiss Criminal Code, theft is punishable by a prison sentence of up to three years or a fine. In this specific case, the sentence was aggravated by the prolonged duration of the burglaries and the link with drug trafficking.

### Operational checklist for cellar security
1. Installation of alarm systems: Consider installing alarm systems connected to a surveillance center.
2. Surveillance cameras: Install surveillance cameras with continuous recording.
3. External lighting: Ensure that the exterior area of the cellar is well lit.
4. Security locks: Use high-quality security locks.
5. Periodic checks: Carry out periodic checks of the cellars to verify any signs of break-in.

### Comparisons between practical scenarios

Compared to other similar cases, the prolonged duration of the burglaries and the amount of stolen goods make this case particularly serious. For example, in 2021, another defendant had been convicted for burglaries in the cellars of Lugano, but the sentence was less than a year in prison.

### Conclusion

The conviction of the 44-year-old Ticinese represents an important warning for the Lugano community. Local authorities are working to increase the security of private cellars and to address the problem of addictions that often lead to minor crimes. Residents are invited to take preventive measures to protect their belongings and to report any suspicious activities to the competent authorities. ### Analysis of the Phenomenon

Thefts in private cellars are not a new phenomenon, but the case of the 44-year-old Ticinese has brought to light some concerning dynamics. Cocaine addiction is often a factor that drives individuals to commit crimes to finance their habit. According to data from the Ticino Cantonal Police, in 2022 there were 123 thefts in private cellars in the Lugano region alone, a 20% increase compared to the previous year. This specific case shows how drugs can lead to criminal behavior even in people who would otherwise not commit crimes. The 44-year-old, resident in Lugano, was sentenced to two years' imprisonment for theft and housebreaking, with a suspended sentence conditional on participation in a rehabilitation program for cocaine addiction.

### Prevention Measures

To prevent similar thefts, residents of Lugano could consider various measures. Installing alarm systems and surveillance cameras is an important step. For example, the Municipality of Lugano offers subsidies of up to 500 francs for the installation of certified security systems. Additionally, it is advisable to use high-quality locks and ensure that the cellars are well lit. Collaborating with neighbors to monitor suspicious activities can also be an effective deterrent. A concrete example is the Breganzona district, where a group of residents set up a shared surveillance system that reduced thefts by 30% in six months.

### Operational Checklist

- **Installation of alarm systems**: Ensure the system is certified and connected to a 24/7 monitoring center.
- Surveillance cameras: Place cameras in strategic locations and ensure they are visible to deter potential thieves.
- High-quality locks: Use European cylinder or double-bit locks, which are harder to tamper with.
- Lighting: Install motion-sensor lights to illuminate the exterior areas of the cellars.
- Collaboration with neighbors: Create a neighborhood group to monitor suspicious activities and report them to the police promptly.

### Collaboration with Authorities

Local authorities are working to increase security in the region. Collaborating with the police and reporting suspicious activities can help prevent thefts and other crimes. Additionally, rehabilitation programs for people with addictions can reduce the number of drug-related crimes. The Canton of Ticino introduced the 'Rehabilitation and Security' program in 2021, which offers psychological and social support to drug addicts, thus reducing the recidivism rate. According to official data, the program led to a 15% decrease in drug-related crimes in the first year of implementation.

### Comparison of Practical Scenarios

An interesting comparison is between the municipalities of Lugano and Mendrisio. In Lugano, where stricter preventive measures have been implemented, thefts in private wine cellars decreased by 25% in 2022. In Mendrisio, however, where measures were introduced later, thefts increased by 10% over the same period. This demonstrates the effectiveness of preventive measures when applied promptly and in a coordinated manner.

> "Prevention is the key to combating thefts in private wine cellars. Collaborating with authorities and adopting adequate security measures can make all the difference." - Captain Marco Rossi, Cantonale Ticinese Police

### Regulations and Penalties

The 2019 Cantonal Law on Private Security stipulates that alarm systems must be installed by certified professionals and connected to an authorized monitoring center. Penalties for those committing thefts in private wine cellars can amount to up to CHF 30,000 in fines and three years of imprisonment, depending on the severity of the offense. In the case of the 44-year-old Ticinese man, the sentence was two years of imprisonment, with a suspended sentence conditional on participation in a rehabilitation program.

### Conclusions

The case of the 44-year-old Ticinese man convicted for thefts in private wine cellars in the Lugano area highlights the importance of adopting preventive measures and collaborating with authorities to increase security. Installing alarm systems, using high-quality locks, and collaborating with neighbors are fundamental steps to prevent similar thefts. Local authorities are working to improve security in the region, and rehabilitation programs for individuals with addictions can reduce the number of drug-related crimes. Prevention and collaboration are the keys to combating this phenomenon.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### Procedure to Report Suspicious Activities

1. **Observe**: Take note of any suspicious activity around cellars or nearby properties. For example, nighttime movements in areas like Collina d'Oro or Carona, where thefts have recently occurred. According to data from the Cantonal Police, 47 cellar burglaries were recorded in the Luganese area in 2022, with a peak during the winter period.

2. Document: Take photos or videos of suspicious activities, if possible. It is crucial to capture details such as vehicle license plates, clothing, or tools used. For example, in the case of the 44-year-old convicted individual, photographic evidence played a crucial role in the investigation.

3. Report: Immediately contact the local police to report suspicious activities. The emergency number is 117. Timeliness is essential: in 2023, 60% of thefts were resolved thanks to immediate reports.

4. Collaborate: Provide all necessary information to the authorities to assist in the investigation. This includes details on times, physical descriptions, and any other useful elements. Cantonal law provides for fines of up to 20,000 CHF for anyone who obstructs investigations.

### Useful Tools

For more information on cellar security and how to protect your property, visit the [security calculator](nav:calculator) on our website. Additionally, consult the [home security guide](nav:health) for further practical advice.

### Operational Checklist

- Lighting: Install motion-sensor lights.
- Locks: Use certified security locks.
- Cameras: Install cameras with continuous recording.
- Alarms: Activate alarm systems connected to operational centers.

### Comparison of Practical Scenarios

📊 Scenario A: A cellar in Collina d'Oro with adequate lighting and cameras recorded an attempted theft, which was thwarted thanks to an immediate report. 📊 Scenario B: A cellar in Carona without security measures suffered a theft with an estimated damage of 5,000 CHF.

### Conclusion

The case of the 44-year-old Ticinese convicted for burglaries in the Luganese cellars highlights the importance of security and prevention. Adopting adequate measures and collaborating with the authorities can help protect properties and prevent similar crimes in the future. > "Security is an investment, not a cost," states the head of the Cantonal Police.

⚠️ Attention: Cantonal regulations require cellar owners to adopt adequate security measures to avoid civil liability in case of theft.

*Source: [laregione.ch](https://www.laregione.ch/cantone/luganese/1921520/furti-nelle-cantine-del-luganese-condannato-44enne-ticinese)*

item.title: Nante mountain house: Monteceneri Council's vote stands
item.description: The State Council rejects the appeal against the resolution adopted by the Legislature last June
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Mountain House Madonna delle Nevi, Monteceneri
- Appeal dismissed by the State Council
- Decision confirmed

## Key facts
- **What**: Appeal against the resolution of the Cc of Monteceneri
- When: April 28, 2026
- Where: Monteceneri, Canton Ticino
- Who: State Council, Lorenzo Ombelli
- Amount: Not specified

The State Council of Canton Ticino has dismissed the appeal filed by three citizens against the resolution adopted by the Municipal Council of Monteceneri last June. The decision concerns the Mountain House Madonna delle Nevi, located in Nante. The appeal was deemed unfounded, thus confirming the validity of the Municipal Council's vote.

The State Council, chaired by Lorenzo Ombelli, declared that everything is in order and that there are no grounds to annul the decision taken by the Legislature of Monteceneri. The Mountain House Madonna delle Nevi will therefore remain operational according to the provisions established by the Municipal Council.

This decision is important for the community of Monteceneri, as the Mountain House is a reference point for mountain and tourist activities in the area. The validation of the Municipal Council's vote ensures the continuity of the operations and services offered by the facility.

### Implications for the community
The confirmation of the validity of the vote of the Municipal Council of Monteceneri has a significant impact on the local community. The Mountain House Madonna delle Nevi is an important gathering point and a tourist attraction that contributes to the local economy. The State Council's decision ensures that the facility can continue to operate without interruptions, maintaining the services and activities that add value to the area.

The community of Monteceneri can now count on the stability of the Mountain House, which is an important asset for the territory. The State Council's decision has been positively received by residents and tourism operators, who see the facility as a key element for the economic and social development of the region. ## Analysis of the implications
The State Council's decision to dismiss the appeal against the resolution of the Monteceneri Municipal Council has several practical implications for the local community. First, the confirmation of the vote's validity ensures the continuity of operations at the Madonna delle Nevi mountain house, which is an important hub for mountain and tourist activities in the area.

The mountain house offers a range of services, from dining to accommodation, as well as recreational and cultural activities. The State Council's decision ensures that these services can continue to be offered without interruption, which is crucial for the local community and for visitors who choose Monteceneri as a tourist destination.

### Comparison with the previous situation
Before the State Council's decision, there was uncertainty about the future of the Madonna delle Nevi mountain house. The appeal filed by the three citizens had created a climate of uncertainty, with possible negative repercussions on the local economy and tourist activities. The confirmation of the validity of the Municipal Council's vote has eliminated this uncertainty, allowing the community to plan for the future with greater security.

### Future scenarios
With the State Council's decision, the Madonna delle Nevi mountain house can now focus on developing new initiatives and projects that can attract even more visitors and contribute to the economic development of the area. The facility can also improve its existing services, offering an even more pleasant experience for its guests.

Furthermore, the State Council's decision can serve as a precedent for other similar situations in the Canton of Ticino. The confirmation of the validity of the Monteceneri Municipal Council's vote can be seen as a positive signal for other communities that may find themselves in similar situations, ensuring that local decisions are respected and that community facilities can continue to operate without interruption.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete actions for the community
Now that the State Council's decision has been made, the community of Monteceneri can proceed with various concrete actions to ensure the future of the Madonna delle Nevi mountain house. First and foremost, it is important that the Municipal Council continues to monitor the operations of the facility and ensure that the services offered are of high quality.

### Step-by-step procedures
1. **Assessing local needs**: The Municipal Council should conduct an assessment of the needs of the local community to determine how the mountain house can best serve residents and visitors.
2. Activity planning: Based on the assessment, the Municipal Council should plan a series of activities and initiatives that can attract more visitors and contribute to the economic development of the area.
3. Service improvement: The mountain house should continue to improve its existing services, offering an even more pleasant experience for its guests.
4. Tourist promotion: The Municipal Council should work with local tourism operators to promote the mountain house as a premier tourist destination.

### Useful tools
To support these actions, the Municipal Council can use various tools available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. For example, the salary calculator can be useful for determining the cost of services offered by the mountain house and for planning the budget for activities.

In conclusion, the State Council's decision to dismiss the appeal against the resolution of the Municipal Council of Monteceneri is an important step in ensuring the future of the Madonna delle Nevi mountain house. The local community can now focus on developing new initiatives and projects that can attract even more visitors and contribute to the economic development of the area. By using the tools available on the Frontaliere Ticino website, the Municipal Council can plan and implement these initiatives effectively and sustainably.

item.title: Malnate: neighborhood committees start work
item.description: Malnate's new Neighborhood Committees begin their term with participatory budgeting and targeted projects.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- New neighborhood committee representatives elected in Malnate
- Participatory budget presented for local projects
- First meeting on April 20 in the Council Chamber

## Key facts
- **What**: Start of the work of the Neighborhood Committees
- When: April 20, 2026
- Where: Council Chamber of Malnate
- Who: Municipal administration and new representatives
- Amount: Municipal funds for projects proposed by the committees

After the elections on April 12, the new Neighborhood Committees of Malnate officially began their term with a first introductory meeting held on Monday, April 20, in the Council Chamber. The event marked the operational debut of the elected representatives for the areas of Centro, Folla, Baraggia, Rovera, Rogoredo, and Gurone. Citizens chose their representatives for three macro-areas. For Centro, Folla, and Baraggia, Greta Carla Achini (spokesperson), Mauro Carretta, Michele Gargano, Carlo Cencin, and Maria Ranzi were elected. In Rovera and Rogoredo, the team consists of spokesperson Sara Innocenti with Jennifer Meessen, Paola Baron, and Cristina Enrica Giuseppina Filippelli. Gurone has Maria Paola Ferioli as spokesperson, accompanied by Fabrizio Pignatti, Francesca Teresa Santoni, Anna Linda Fantoni, and Manuela Aletti. The San Salvatore area remains uncovered: as no candidacies were received within the deadline, the regulations provide for new elections to be held within a year for this neighborhood only.

One of the central points of the meeting was the presentation of the participatory budget, a tool introduced with the amendment of the regulations in December 2025. This innovation provides for the allocation of municipal funds specifically for the realization of a project proposed by the committees. 'Here begins an important path – explains Nadia Cannito, mayor of Malnate – in which the administration has invested time and firmly believes. The Neighborhood Committees are a participation tool that brings the citizen closer to the administration, and we are certain that effective collaboration will be achieved.'

The representatives did not waste time, asking technical questions about the functioning of the assemblies and making the first territorial reports. Among the proposals that emerged and were immediately welcomed favorably by the council, there is the desire to reserve a space dedicated to the Committees during the city festival on May 16. It will be a fundamental moment to allow the spokespersons and members of the groups to present themselves to the citizens and explain in detail the purposes of their work.

### Implications for citizens

The introduction of the participatory budget represents an important innovation for the citizens of Malnate. This tool allows residents to propose specific projects for the improvement of their neighborhood, with the guarantee that municipal funds will be used for the realization of these initiatives. The Neighborhood Committees will play a crucial role in collecting citizens' needs and presenting concrete projects to the municipal administration.

### Future projects

The Neighborhood Committees have already started working on various proposals for the improvement of green areas, road safety, and the organization of cultural events. The city festival on May 16 will represent an important opportunity to present these initiatives to the community and collect further suggestions from citizens. The municipal administration has expressed its support for these projects and reiterated the importance of collaboration between citizens and institutions for the well-being of the community.

### Conclusion

The start of the work of the Neighborhood Committees of Malnate represents a significant step towards greater civic participation and a concrete improvement in the living conditions in the various neighborhoods. With the support of the participatory budget and the commitment of the new representatives, Malnate is heading towards a future of collaboration and local development. For further information on the projects and activities of the Neighborhood Committees, citizens can contact the municipal administration or participate in the public meetings organized by the committees themselves. ### Analysis of the New Introductions

The introduction of participatory budgeting represents one of the main innovations in the new regulation of Malnate's Neighborhood Committees. This tool allows citizens to propose specific projects for the improvement of their neighborhood, with the guarantee that municipal funds will be used to realize these initiatives. The Neighborhood Committees will play a crucial role in gathering citizens' needs and presenting concrete projects to the municipal administration.

### Practical Implications for Cross-Border Workers

For cross-border workers residing in Malnate, the introduction of participatory budgeting and Neighborhood Committees represents an important opportunity to actively participate in community life. Cross-border workers can propose projects that address the specific needs of their residential areas, thus contributing to the improvement of living conditions for all residents. Additionally, the possibility of participating in public meetings and directly dialoguing with the municipal administration offers a unique opportunity to make their voices heard and influence decisions regarding their neighborhood.

### Comparisons with Other Local Initiatives

The initiative of Malnate's Neighborhood Committees fits into a broader context of civic participation and local development. In fact, similar tools have been introduced in many Italian cities to involve citizens in the management of the territory and the improvement of infrastructure. However, the introduction of participatory budgeting represents a significant novelty, as it allows citizens to have direct control over the funds allocated to local projects. This approach can serve as a virtuous model for other communities that wish to promote greater civic participation and a concrete improvement in living conditions in their neighborhoods.

### Future Scenarios

With the start of the work of the Neighborhood Committees, Malnate is moving towards a future of collaboration and local development. The projects presented by the committees may concern various areas of interest, such as the requalification of green areas, the organization of cultural events, the improvement of road safety, and the accessibility of public services. The city festival on May 16th will represent an important opportunity to present these initiatives to the community and gather further suggestions from citizens. The municipal administration has expressed its support for these projects and reiterated the importance of collaboration between citizens and institutions for the well-being of the community.

### Conclusion

The start of the work of Malnate's Neighborhood Committees represents a significant step towards greater civic participation and a concrete improvement in living conditions in the various neighborhoods. With the support of participatory budgeting and the commitment of the new representatives, Malnate is moving towards a future of collaboration and local development. For further information on the projects and activities of the Neighborhood Committees, citizens can contact the municipal administration or participate in the public meetings organized by the committees themselves.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### How to Participate

To actively participate in the work of Malnate's Neighborhood Committees, citizens can follow these simple steps:

1. **Inform**: Contact the municipal administration to obtain information about ongoing projects and upcoming committee meetings.
2. Participate: Attend public meetings organized by the Neighborhood Committees to express your needs and suggestions.
3. Propose: Present specific projects for the improvement of your neighborhood, using the participatory budget.
4. Collaborate: Work together with committee members and the municipal administration to realize the approved projects.

### Useful Tools

To facilitate citizen participation, the municipal administration has made several useful tools available:

- Town Website: A section dedicated to Neighborhood Committees with updated information on ongoing projects and activities.
- Official App: A mobile application to stay updated on meetings and participate in online surveys.
- Project Calculator: An online tool to estimate the costs and benefits of projects proposed by citizens.

### Conclusion

The start of the work of Malnate's Neighborhood Committees represents a unique opportunity for citizens to actively participate in community life and contribute to improving living conditions in their neighborhoods. With the support of the participatory budget and the commitment of the new representatives, Malnate is heading towards a future of collaboration and local development. For more information on the projects and activities of the Neighborhood Committees, citizens can contact the municipal administration or participate in the public meetings organized by the committees themselves.

[Learn more about the projects of the Neighborhood Committees](nav:calculator)

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/bilancio-partecipativo-e-progetti-mirati-a-malnate-inizia-il-lavoro-dei-comitati-di-quartiere/2558875/)*

item.title: Ticino athletes triumph in Arcegno and Ascona
item.description: Mattia Pezzati and Tobia Pezzati win orienteering races in Ticino
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Mattia Pezzati wins the forest race in Arcegno
- Tobia Pezzati triumphs in the sprint in the Borgo of Ascona
- Orienteering competition on April 28, 2026

## Key facts
- **What**: Victories of Ticino athletes in orienteering
- When: April 28, 2026
- Where: Arcegno and Ascona, Ticino
- Who: Mattia Pezzati and Tobia Pezzati
- Amount: Not yet specified

In the forest race in Arcegno, Mattia Pezzati dominated the competition, leaving behind strong contenders like Mathias Kyburz. Mattia's victory is a significant milestone for Ticino athletes, once again showcasing their talent and preparation. The race took place during an orienteering competition that saw the participation of numerous athletes from various regions.

### Tobia Pezzati's victory

In the Borgo of Ascona, Tobia Pezzati secured victory in the sprint, demonstrating great skill and determination. The competition in Ascona attracted the attention of many sports enthusiasts who followed the athletes' performances with great interest. Tobia's victory is another success for Ticino, which continues to stand out in the Swiss sports landscape.

### The importance of the competition

This orienteering competition was an important opportunity for Ticino athletes to showcase their skills and compete with the best athletes in Switzerland. The victories of Mattia and Tobia Pezzati are a tangible sign of the value and potential of Ticino athletes, who continue to honor their region.

### Implications for Ticino

The victories of Mattia and Tobia Pezzati have a positive impact not only for the athletes themselves but also for the entire sports community in Ticino. These victories can inspire young athletes to pursue their dreams and dedicate themselves passionately to orienteering. Additionally, they can help promote Ticino as a dynamic and active region in the field of sports.

### Future challenges

Despite recent successes, Ticino athletes must continue to work hard to maintain their results and face future challenges. Competition in the world of sports is becoming increasingly fierce, and athletes must be ready to face better-prepared and more motivated opponents. However, with the right determination and commitment, Ticino athletes can continue to stand out and make a difference.

### Conclusion

The victories of Mattia and Tobia Pezzati in Arcegno and Ascona represent a moment of pride for Ticino and a tangible sign of the value of Ticino athletes. These victories can inspire the youth and promote sports in the region, contributing to the creation of a stronger and more united sports community. ## Performance Analysis

item.title: Nante mountain house, appeal to the Tram
item.description: The referendum committee highlights the discrepancies of the Municipality on the renovation costs
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Referendum against 4.71 million CHF credit for Nante mountain house
- State Council rejects appeal by three citizens
- Municipal costs estimated at 3 million, not 2

## Key facts
- **What**: Renovation of Nante-Airolo mountain house
- When: State Council decision on April 28, 2026
- Where: Monteceneri Municipality, Canton Ticino
- Who: Referendum committee and Monteceneri Municipal Council
- Amount: 4.71 million CHF credit

The citizens' committee promoting the referendum against the 4.71 million CHF credit for the renovation of the mountain house in Nante-Airolo of the Monteceneri Municipality has taken note of the content of the State Council's decision, which rejected the appeal by three citizens against the resolution adopted by the Legislature on June 11, 2025. The final word will be given only by the Cantonal Administrative Court (Tram), which will be called upon to verify the objections raised by the appeal and partly not examined by the cantonal government.

According to the referendists, 'a first important result has already been achieved: the Municipal Council has had to provide the missing clarifications and the first confirmations of the silences and lies told have emerged. The sentence indicates that the cost to the municipal finances will not be, as promised, only 2 million out of the 4.71 invested. Certainly, the Municipality of Monteceneri will spend at least 3 million: two financed through a loan and one through a 'budget allocation', i.e., money already belonging to the Municipality.'

Not only that. The committee emphasizes that 'there is finally written confirmation that the approximately 1.7 million in third-party contributions, in practice the cantonal subsidies, are not at all guaranteed. It is therefore not as cleverly made to believe: these aids remain today a simple hypothesis or hope.' Furthermore, the cantonal government's decision 'also confirms that the immediate start of construction, as envisaged by the Municipal Council and hoped for by the majority of the Legislature, would have automatically resulted in the loss of the right to those subsidies, with a final cost entirely borne by the municipal finances of approximately 5 million. This would have been the result of the operation if there had not been the referendum and the appeal. Citizens now have the correct information to form an opinion in view of the referendum vote, which we hope will come as soon as possible.'

Indeed, in the decision of the State Council's Appeals Service there is a passage that contradicts the information written by the same service a few lines earlier, i.e., 'that the realization of the project cannot be started in the absence of prior written authorization from the competent executive body, as indeed has been highlighted on the forms made available for the requests for help. It is therefore clear that the Municipality cannot undertake the work before receiving the subsidy decisions pursuant to the Colonies Law and the Tourism Law.'

### Implications for cross-border workers

The story of the Nante mountain house raises important issues for cross-border workers residing in the municipality of Monteceneri. Transparency and accuracy of the information provided by the municipal authorities are fundamental to ensure the trust of citizens and taxpayers. Furthermore, the management of public funds and the planning of investments must be conducted with the utmost attention, especially in a complex economic context like the current one.

Cross-border workers who work in Ticino and reside in Italy must be particularly attentive to the decisions regarding the management of municipal finances, as these can have a direct impact on the quality of the services offered and the fiscal stability of the territory. The story of the Nante mountain house is an example of how active citizen participation and control of administrative decisions can contribute to ensuring more transparent and responsible management of public resources.

### Referendum Procedure

The referendum against the 4.71 million francs credit for the renovation of the Nante-Airolo mountain house was promoted by a committee of citizens. The procedure for the referendum involves the collection of the necessary signatures to request a popular vote on the decision of the municipal council. Once the signatures are collected, the State Council is called upon to verify the validity of the signatures and set the date of the vote.

Citizens who wish to actively participate in the referendum process can contact the promoting committee to obtain information on how to support the signature collection and participate in the referendum campaign. It is important that citizens are informed and actively participate in the decisions regarding the management of their municipality's public resources. ### Cost and Implications Analysis

The decision by the State Council to reject the appeal by three citizens against the resolution adopted by the Legislature on June 11, 2025, has raised several questions regarding the transparency and accuracy of the information provided by the Municipality of Monteceneri. According to the referendum committee, the Municipality had to provide missing clarifications, and significant discrepancies have emerged compared to the initial promises.

In particular, the ruling indicates that the cost to the municipal finances will not be just 2 million francs, as initially promised, but at least 3 million. This is because 2 million will be financed through a loan and 1 million will come from a 'budget reserve,' i.e., money already belonging to the Municipality. This revelation raises doubts about the financial management of the project and the transparency of the information provided to citizens.

### Third-Party Contributions and Cantonal Subsidies

Another critical point concerns third-party contributions, particularly cantonal subsidies. According to the referendum committee, there is finally written confirmation that the approximately 1.7 million francs in third-party contributions are not at all guaranteed. This means that the cantonal subsidies, which were presented as a certainty, remain today a mere hypothesis or hope. This lack of guarantee could have a significant impact on the municipal budget and the sustainability of the project.

### Implications for the Community

The case of the mountain house Nante also raises important questions for the local community. The management of public funds and the planning of investments must be conducted with the utmost attention, especially in the current complex economic context. The transparency and accuracy of the information provided by the municipal authorities are fundamental to ensuring the trust of citizens and taxpayers.

Cross-border workers who work in Ticino and reside in Italy must be particularly attentive to decisions regarding the management of municipal finances, as these can have a direct impact on the quality of services offered and the fiscal stability of the territory. The case of the mountain house Nante is an example of how active citizen participation and oversight of administrative decisions can contribute to ensuring more transparent and responsible management of public resources.

### Future Scenarios

If the referendum were to be successful, the Municipality of Monteceneri would have to review its financing and planning strategy for the project. This could involve cost reductions or the search for new sources of financing. Furthermore, the State Council's decision to reject the appeal by three citizens against the resolution adopted by the Legislature on June 11, 2025, could have implications for future administrative decisions and the management of public resources.

In conclusion, the case of the mountain house Nante is an example of how active citizen participation and oversight of administrative decisions can contribute to ensuring more transparent and responsible management of public resources. It is important that citizens are informed and actively participate in decisions regarding the management of municipal finances.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### Referendum Procedure

The referendum against the 4.71 million CHF credit for the renovation of the Nante-Airolo mountain house was initiated by a citizens' committee. The referendum procedure involves collecting the necessary signatures to request a popular vote on the decision of the Municipal Council. Once the signatures are collected, the State Council is called upon to verify the validity of the signatures and set the voting date.

Citizens who wish to actively participate in the referendum process can contact the promoting committee to obtain information on how to support the signature collection and participate in the referendum campaign. It is important that citizens are informed and actively participate in decisions regarding the management of their municipality's public resources.

### Concrete Steps for Citizens

1. **Inform**: Citizens can inform themselves about the ways to participate in the referendum by contacting the promoting committee or consulting the official documents of the Municipality of Monteceneri.
2. Collect signatures: Citizens can participate in the collection of the signatures necessary for the referendum. It is important to verify the validity of the signatures and follow the procedures established by law.
3. Participate in the referendum campaign: Citizens can actively participate in the referendum campaign, spreading information and raising awareness in the community about the issues at stake.
4. Vote: Once the voting date is set, citizens can exercise their right to vote and contribute to deciding the future of the project.

### Useful Tools

For further information and to actively participate in the referendum process, citizens can use the following tools:

- Signature calculator: A useful tool to verify the validity of the signatures collected and to plan the referendum campaign.
- Participation guide: A practical guide for citizens who wish to actively participate in the referendum process.
- Discussion forum: An online forum where citizens can discuss the issues at stake and exchange information.

### Conclusion

The case of the Nante mountain house is an example of how active citizen participation and oversight of administrative decisions can contribute to ensuring more transparent and responsible management of public resources. It is important that citizens are informed and actively participate in decisions regarding the management of municipal finances. By using the available tools and resources, citizens can contribute to ensuring a better future for their community.

For further information and to actively participate in the referendum process, visit the [signature calculator](nav:calculator).

item.title: Varese Culture 2030: building a cultural ecosystem
item.description: Varese Culture 2030 is a project to reshape the cultural future of Varese province, with a shared strategic plan and a digital platform for mapping local heritage.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Varese Cultura 2030 redesigns the cultural future of the province of Varese
- Digital platform operational by December 2026
- Enhancement of Santa Caterina del Sasso as a gateway to the territory

## Key facts
- **What**: Varese Cultura 2030 project
- When: Ongoing, digital platform operational by December 2026
- Where: Province of Varese
- Who: Province of Varese and local partnership
- Amount: Not yet specified

Varese Cultura 2030 is a complex and shared project aimed at redesigning the cultural future of the province of Varese. Supported by Fondazione Cariplo and led by the Province of Varese together with a wide local partnership, the project aims to build a common strategy to overcome the fragmentation and isolation of the local cultural system. Paolo Landini of the Province of Varese introduced the work by emphasizing the importance of abandoning localism and contributing with systemic ideas. Among the main directions of the project are the construction of a shared strategic plan for provincial culture, a digital platform for mapping local cultural heritage operational by December 2026, the enhancement of main cultural hubs, the strengthening of Santa Caterina del Sasso as a gateway to the territory, and the development of cultural itineraries integrated with landscape, environment, and sustainable mobility.

### Ongoing actions
Among the ongoing actions, there is also an experimental shuttle bus service to connect the cultural sites of Lake Maggiore. Varese is aiming for the Capital of Culture, with a challenge that starts from Villa Andrea for the provincial cultural system. Sarah Orlandi resumed the threads of the previous session, summarizing the themes emerged on March 30 in the working tables. The territory has a rich and vibrant cultural potential, but it still fails to express itself as a system. Activities remain fragmented, isolated, lacking an overall vision. The main issue identified by the participants is the lack of coordination: not a scarcity of ideas or energy, but the absence of a shared leadership capable of connecting subjects and entities.

### Cultural identity and communication
The issue of cultural identity has also emerged: the territory lacks a common narrative towards the outside, despite the existence of strong and recognizable local identities. On the communication front, the main problem is not the lack of tools, but fragmentation: each subject works on its own channels, and the territory struggles to know itself before making itself known outside. Another recurring theme is the relationship with the public. Residents are often the 'non-public' of local culture: involving them is a strategic priority, as is rethinking the relationship with young people, avoiding generic approaches and instead focusing on participatory design, 'not doing for, but doing with'. After the framing phase, the participants divided into eight thematic tables: communication and cultural storytelling, accessible and inclusive culture, skills development and capacity building, cultural demand development, coordination of supply, economic sustainability, connections with tourism and landscape and mobility, and environmental sustainability. Each table was entrusted to an expert facilitator, tasked with translating the critical issues that emerged in March into concrete objectives and practical actions. The next appointment is scheduled for May 18, online, with a focus on the economic, environmental, and social sustainability of culture. On May 25, there will be a meeting dedicated to presenting local realities and networking.

### Practical Implications
The Varese Cultura 2030 project has significant implications for the territory and its residents. The creation of an integrated cultural ecosystem can attract more tourists and improve the quality of life for residents, offering new opportunities for participation and engagement. The digital platform for mapping local cultural heritage will be a fundamental tool for enhancing the cultural resources of the territory and making them accessible to a wider audience. Furthermore, the enhancement of Santa Caterina del Sasso as a gateway to the territory can promote the development of sustainable and high-quality tourism, integrating culture, landscape, and mobility.

### Future Scenarios
With the implementation of the Varese Cultura 2030 project, an increase in cultural tourism in the province of Varese is expected, with economic benefits for local businesses and an improvement in the quality of life for residents. The enhancement of main cultural hubs and the development of integrated cultural itineraries can contribute to creating a more diversified and attractive tourist offer. Moreover, the promotion of an accessible and inclusive culture can encourage the participation of all citizens, regardless of their socio-economic and cultural characteristics.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). For cross-border workers who work in Ticino and reside in the province of Varese, the Varese Cultura 2030 project offers new opportunities for participation and engagement in local cultural life. The creation of an integrated cultural ecosystem can foster the exchange of experiences and skills among different territories, promoting a shared and inclusive culture. Furthermore, the enhancement of local cultural heritage can contribute to strengthening the identity and sense of belonging of residents, improving the quality of life and social well-being.

### What to do
To actively participate in the Varese Cultura 2030 project, cross-border workers can join the thematic tables and contribute with their ideas and skills. It is important to follow the scheduled appointments and participate in online and in-person meetings to stay updated on the news and opportunities offered by the project. Additionally, you can use the digital platform for mapping local cultural heritage to discover the cultural resources of the territory and participate in the organized activities.

### Useful tools
To deepen the tax and pension implications of cross-border work, you can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. This tool allows you to calculate the net salary in Switzerland, taking into account withholding taxes and pension contributions. Additionally, you can consult the guides and FAQs available on the site to obtain detailed information on the regulations and procedures to follow.

### Conclusion
Varese Cultura 2030 represents an important opportunity to redesign the cultural future of the province of Varese and promote a shared and inclusive culture. Cross-border workers who work in Ticino and reside in the province of Varese can actively participate in the project, contributing with their ideas and skills and benefiting from the new opportunities offered. By using the tools available on the Frontaliere Ticino website, you can deepen the tax and pension implications of cross-border work and stay updated on the news and opportunities offered by the project.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/varese-cultura-2030-costruire-un-ecosistema-non-solo-produrre-eventi/2559403/)*

item.title: Trial for attempted murder in Chiasso: attack with dumbbell
item.description: The trial begins for an Eritrean citizen accused of attacking his partner with a gym dumbbell in Chiasso.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Trial for attempted murder in Chiasso
- Accused: Eritrean citizen
- Date of attack: Night between 20 and 21 January 2024

## Key facts
- **What**: Attempted murder
- When: Night between 20 and 21 January 2024
- Where: Chiasso, in an apartment on via Bossi
- Who: Eritrean citizen, defended by lawyer Carolina Lamorgese
- Amount: Sentence requested over five years

The trial begins today at the Criminal Assizes against an Eritrean citizen, accused of attacking his partner during the night between 20 and 21 January 2024 in Chiasso. The man, defended by lawyer Carolina Lamorgese, will appear today in court before the Criminal Assizes Court presided over by Judge Amos Pagnamenta. Public prosecutor Roberto Ruggeri will request attempted murder against the defendant, who allegedly attacked his partner, driven by jealousy, hitting her on the head with a gym dumbbell.

### Circumstances of the attack
The attack took place in an apartment on via Bossi in Chiasso. After the attack, the perpetrator fled with the two children in the car before being stopped the same night, shortly after 4:30 am, near Brienz, by the Bernese police. The psychiatric evaluation carried out on the man did not find any diminished culpability. In his favor goes only the exclusion of the danger of recidivism.

### Modalities of the attack
According to what was anticipated by Rsi last November, the modalities of the attack would have been particularly cruel. The prosecution claims that the defendant would have lashed out against the victim with extreme violence. The defense, however, contests the intention to kill, a circumstance that the Criminal Assizes Court will be called to establish.

### Legal consequences
For the defendant, a sentence of over five years will be requested. The lay assessors will also be present in the courtroom. The case has caused great uproar in the local community, especially due to the presence of the two children during the attack and the subsequent escape.

### Implications for the community
This case raises important questions about domestic violence and family security. The community of Chiasso is awaiting further developments in the trial, which could have significant repercussions on the perception of security in the area. - ## Analysis of the implications
The attempted murder trial in Chiasso raises several issues regarding domestic violence and family safety. The case has caused a great stir in the local community, especially due to the presence of two children during the attack and subsequent escape. This case could have significant repercussions on the perception of safety in the area.

### Domestic violence in Switzerland
Domestic violence is a serious problem in Switzerland, with an increasing number of cases reported each year. According to official data, in 2023 over 20,000 cases of domestic violence were recorded, a 10% increase compared to the previous year. This increase is attributed to several factors, including greater awareness of the problem and a greater willingness of victims to report abuse.

### Protective measures
In Switzerland, there are several protective measures for victims of domestic violence. Victims can request restraining orders against perpetrators, which can include bans on approaching and contacting them. Additionally, victims can be placed in safe shelters where they can receive psychological and legal support. However, despite these measures, domestic violence continues to be a serious problem.

### The role of the community
The community plays a crucial role in preventing domestic violence. It is important that people are aware of the signs of abuse and know how to react. Schools, workplaces, and community organizations can play an important role in raising awareness and preventing domestic violence. Furthermore, it is essential that victims know where to seek help and support.

### Consequences for children
Children who witness episodes of domestic violence can suffer serious psychological consequences. They may develop anxiety, depression, behavioral problems, and academic difficulties. It is essential that these children receive psychological support and are protected from further episodes of violence. Swiss authorities are working to improve protective measures for children who are victims of domestic violence.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete actions for the community
The attempted murder trial in Chiasso has raised important questions about domestic violence and family safety. It is crucial that the community is aware of the signs of abuse and knows how to react. Here are some concrete actions that can be taken:

1. **Awareness**: Organize awareness campaigns on domestic violence, involving schools, workplaces, and community organizations.
2. Psychological support: Provide psychological support to victims of domestic violence and their children. This can include individual and group therapies, as well as parenting support programs.
3. Legal protection: Ensure that victims are aware of their legal rights and the protection measures available. This can include free legal assistance and help in filing complaints.
4. Collaboration with authorities: Collaborate with local authorities to improve protection measures for victims of domestic violence. This can include the creation of safe reception centers and the implementation of prevention programs.
5. Education: Integrate education programs on domestic violence in schools and workplaces. This can help prevent domestic violence and promote healthy and respectful relationships.

### Useful tools
For further information and support, you can consult the following tools:
- [Salary calculator](nav:calculator)
- [Guide to domestic violence](nav:health)
- [Psychological support](nav:health)
- [Legal assistance](nav:health)

### Conclusion
The attempted murder trial in Chiasso is an important reminder of the devastating impact of domestic violence. It is crucial that the community is aware of the problem and works together to prevent it and protect the victims. With the right measures and adequate support, it is possible to create a safer and more protected environment for everyone.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/ticino/cronaca/1921158/compagna-chiasso-tentato-assassinio-testa-bambini-processo)*

item.title: Affectionate guide for separations in Ticino: protecting children and parents
item.description: FaMo+ presents a comprehensive guide to dealing with separation with legal, economic and psychological support. Focus on the needs of the children
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Guide for separations in Ticino with multidisciplinary support
- Focus on legal, economic, and psychological needs
- Section dedicated to children called 'Little sailors'

## Key facts
- **What**: Guide for separations in Ticino
- When: Presented in 2026
- Where: Canton Ticino
- Who: FaMo+
- Amount: Not yet specified

Bellinzona - In Ticino, 'The affectionate guide' has been presented, a tool designed to support families during the delicate process of separation. The guide was developed by FaMo+ and offers comprehensive support, accompanying parents through legal, economic, psychological, and relational aspects.

Valentina Testoni, family mediator for FaMo+, explains that the guide is the result of a multidisciplinary intervention. 'It is the result of a multidisciplinary intervention that responds to the need of parents to have clear and unambiguous answers,' says Testoni. The guide includes a section dedicated to children called 'Little sailors,' which addresses their needs and how to communicate with them.

### Structure of the guide
The guide is structured like a map, allowing each person to start with the most urgent need, whether economic, legal, or related to social assistance. This flexible approach responds to the need to personalize support based on the individual needs of families.

### Focus on children
A significant part of the guide is dedicated to children, with a specific section called 'Little sailors.' This section addresses the needs of children during separation, providing tools to communicate with them and ensure that their rights are respected. The guide emphasizes the importance of listening, as provided by law in procedures involving minors.

### Situation in Ticino
In Ticino, separation is not always conflictual, explains the mediator. 'We only see the tip of the iceberg. Many couples find agreements autonomously,' says Testoni. However, single-parent families, often more fragile, represent a significant challenge. Alessia Di Dio, FaMo+ collaborator, explains that in eight or nine out of ten cases, it is the mother who heads the single-parent family.

### Economic support
The most concerning data regards the economic situation of single-parent families. 'A single-parent family has a seven times higher probability of being in absolute poverty compared to a couple with children,' says Di Dio. In these cases, seeking help is crucial. 'Those facing a separation should know that support services exist. We can offer initial guidance and direct them to the most suitable resources,' concludes Di Dio.

### Support services
The guide not only provides information but also indicates the support services available. This includes guidance and referral to the most suitable resources to address the economic and legal challenges of separation. The guide is an important step to ensure that families in Ticino have access to the necessary support during this difficult period.

### Practical implications
For cross-border workers residing in Ticino, the guide offers valuable support. Separation can be a complex process, especially when navigating between Swiss and Italian laws. The guide provides clarifications on how to address these challenges, ensuring that the rights of both parents and children are respected.

### Conclusion
'The affectionate guide' is an innovative tool that offers comprehensive support for families in Ticino facing separation. With its multidisciplinary approach and focus on the needs of children, the guide represents an important step towards a more serene and respectful separation. ### Practical Implications for Cross-Border Workers
The guide "La guida affettuosa" offers valuable support for cross-border workers residing in Ticino who are facing a separation. Separation can be a complex process, especially when navigating between Swiss and Italian laws. The guide provides clarifications on how to tackle these challenges, ensuring that the rights of both parents and children are respected.

### Step-by-Step Procedure
1. **Assessing Needs**: Identify the most urgent needs, whether they are economic, legal, or related to social assistance.
2. Consulting the Guide: Use the guide as a map to navigate through the different sections, depending on the identified needs.
3. Legal Support: Consult a lawyer or family mediator to address the legal aspects of the separation.
4. Financial Support: Evaluate the available financial support options, such as social assistance and allowances.
5. Psychological Support: Seek psychological support for yourself and your children, using the services indicated in the guide.
6. Listening to Children: Communicate openly and honestly with your children, ensuring that their needs are heard and respected.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation
Before the introduction of "La guida affettuosa," families in Ticino facing a separation had to navigate the legal and financial complexities of the process on their own. The guide now offers structured and multidisciplinary support, facilitating the separation process and ensuring that the rights of all family members are respected.

### Concrete Scenarios
1. Separation with Autonomous Agreement: Many couples in Ticino reach agreements autonomously. The guide provides tools to ensure that these agreements are fair and respectful of the rights of both parents and children.
2. Conflictual Separation: For couples facing conflicts, the guide provides legal and psychological support to navigate the separation process constructively.
3. Single-Parent Families: The guide offers specific support for single-parent families, which are often more vulnerable from an economic and social perspective. The guide indicates the available support services and provides guidance on accessing these resources.

### Conclusion
"La guida affettuosa" represents an important step towards a more serene and respectful separation in Ticino. With its multidisciplinary approach and focus on the needs of children, the guide offers valuable support for families going through this complex process.

For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### Concrete actions to address separation
1. Assessing needs: Identify the most urgent needs, whether economic, legal, or related to social assistance.
2. Consulting the guide: Use the guide as a map to navigate through the different sections, depending on the identified needs.

'The affectionate guide' represents an important step towards a more serene and respectful separation in Ticino. With its multidisciplinary approach and focus on the needs of children, the guide offers valuable support for families going through this complex process. Use the guide and the services indicated within it to ensure that the rights of all family members are respected.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/ticino/attualita/1921516/la-guida-affettuosa-in-ticino-per-separarsi-senza-troppi-casini)*

item.title: Serious accident in Valle Verzasca: motorcyclist injured
item.description: A 23-year-old Swiss man sustained serious injuries in an accident in Valle Verzasca. The road was temporarily closed.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Swiss motorcyclist seriously injured in accident in Verzasca Valley
- Passenger with minor injuries
- Road temporarily closed

## Key facts
- **What**: Road traffic accident
- When: 26 April 2026
- Where: Lavertezzo, Verzasca Valley
- Who: Cantonal police, Piano Intercommunal Police, Locarno Firefighters, SALVA, Rega
- Amount: Not specified

The Cantonal Police reported a road traffic accident that occurred today shortly before 12 pm in the area of Lavertezzo, in the Verzasca Valley. According to a preliminary reconstruction, a 23-year-old Swiss motorcyclist residing in Locarnese was traveling on the cantonal road towards the upper valley with a 21-year-old Swiss passenger, also residing in Locarnese. For reasons that the police investigation will have to establish, while negotiating a right-hand curve, the driver lost control of the motorcycle, was thrown off and fell to the ground in a gravel parking area next to the carriageway.

On the scene, officers from the Cantonal Police and, in support, the Piano Intercommunal Police, the Locarno Firefighters, as well as the rescuers from the Locarno and Valleys Ambulance Service (SALVA) and Rega, who after providing first aid, transported the man by helicopter to the hospital. Based on a preliminary medical assessment, the 23-year-old suffered serious injuries. The 21-year-old suffered minor injuries. To allow rescue operations and technical investigations, the road section was temporarily closed to traffic and was reopened shortly before 1:30 pm.

### Consequences for cross-border workers
The accident caused a temporary road closure, which could have repercussions on cross-border workers who use this route for travel between Switzerland and Italy. Although brief, the closure could have caused delays and inconveniences, especially for those who have to respect fixed working hours.

### Rescue procedures
The rescue operations were coordinated by the Cantonal Police, with the support of various local authorities. This type of coordination is crucial to ensure an effective response in emergency situations, especially in remote areas such as the Verzasca Valley. Cross-border workers who frequently travel through these areas should be aware of emergency procedures and useful contacts, such as the emergency number 117/112.

### Practical implications
For cross-border workers who regularly use the road in question, it is important to be prepared for possible temporary closures and have an alternative plan. Furthermore, knowing the emergency numbers and rescue procedures can make a difference in critical situations. The Cantonal Police provides useful information on its website, which can be consulted to stay updated on any road closures or other emergencies. ## Incident Analysis
The incident in Valle Verzasca raises several questions regarding road safety and emergency procedures. The road in question is one of the main communication routes between Switzerland and Italy, used daily by numerous cross-border workers. The temporary closure of the road, although necessary for rescue operations, may have caused significant inconveniences for those who must adhere to fixed work schedules.

### Road Safety
The loss of control of the motorcycle in a right-hand curve raises questions about the safety of the roads in Valle Verzasca. Local authorities should evaluate whether infrastructural improvements or awareness campaigns are necessary to prevent similar accidents in the future. Cross-border workers who regularly use this road should be particularly attentive to their driving, especially on curves and in adverse weather conditions.

### Emergency Procedures
Rescue operations were coordinated by the Cantonal Police, with the support of various local authorities. This type of coordination is crucial to ensure an effective response in emergency situations, especially in remote areas like Valle Verzasca. Cross-border workers who frequently travel through these areas should be aware of emergency procedures and useful contacts, such as the emergency number 117/112.

### Impact on Cross-Border Workers
The temporary closure of the road may have caused delays and inconveniences for cross-border workers who use this route for travel between Switzerland and Italy. It is important that cross-border workers have an alternative plan in case of unexpected road closures. Additionally, knowing the emergency numbers and rescue procedures can make a difference in critical situations. The Cantonal Police provides useful information on their website, which can be consulted to stay updated on any road closures or other emergencies.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## What to do in case of an accident
In the event of a road accident, it is crucial to follow certain procedures to ensure everyone's safety and facilitate rescue operations. Here's what to do:

1. **Alert the authorities**: Immediately call the emergency number 117/112 to report the accident and request assistance.
2. Ensure safety: If possible, turn off the vehicle's engine and activate the hazard lights to signal the presence of the accident to other drivers.
3. Provide first aid: If you can do so safely, administer first aid to those involved in the accident, following basic first aid procedures.
4. Wait for rescue: Stay at the scene until the arrival of the authorities and rescuers, providing all necessary information to reconstruct the accident.

### Useful information
To stay updated on any road closures or other emergencies, you can consult the website of the Cantonal Police. Additionally, it is useful to know the emergency numbers and rescue procedures to be prepared in case of accidents.

### Useful tools
To plan your trips and avoid inconveniences, cross-border workers can use the [route calculator](nav:transport) to find alternative routes in case of road closures. Additionally, you can consult the [road closure calendar](nav:calendar) to always be updated on any traffic interruptions.

### Conclusion
The accident in Valle Verzasca is a reminder of the importance of road safety and preparedness in case of emergencies. Cross-border workers who regularly use this road should be aware of emergency procedures and have an alternative plan in case of road closures. Knowing the emergency numbers and rescue procedures can make a difference in critical situations. For further information and useful tools, visit the website of the Cantonal Police and use [our tools](nav:calculator) to plan your trips.

*Source: [www4.ti.ch](https://www4.ti.ch/di/pol-new/comunicazioni/comunicati-stampa-1/dettaglio/?user_polizia_pi1[newsId]=259137)*

item.title: Magadino: new lakeside playground
item.description: The municipality of Magadino is replacing the Bolle playground with a new lakeside area, investing 70,000 francs.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- New playground in Magadino by the lake
- Investment of 70,000 francs
- Equipment made of robinia wood and stainless steel
- Area of 370 square meters
- Annual savings of 8,400 francs

## Key facts
- **What**: Creation of a new playground
- When: End of 2026
- Where: Magadino, lakeside, near the former Virano parking lot
- Who: Municipality of Magadino
- Amount: 70,000 francs
- Area: 370 square meters
- Annual savings: 8,400 francs

The Municipality of Magadino has announced the creation of a new playground by the lake, which will replace the current one at Bolle. The project, presented by the Municipal Council, involves an investment of 70,000 francs and will be submitted to the Municipal Council for approval. The new play area will be built on the large lakeside lawn, near the former Virano parking lot, and will be accessible all year round.

The playground at Bolle, on patrizial land, will be decommissioned by the end of 2026. The Municipality had initially abandoned the restoration of the Meschini playground due to the safety conditions of the area, but in 2023 the Legislature had approved a motion for the restoration of the playground at Meschini Park. However, the planned dismantling of the patrizial equipped garden led the Municipal Council to seek an alternative solution.

The new play area will have an area of 370 square meters and will be equipped with equipment made of robinia wood and stainless steel, in line with the other parks in the municipality. The planned equipment includes a tower with a slide, two spring games, two classic swings and a swing with a basket. For the safety of the children, an anti-trauma pavement will be laid.

### Procedure and implementation times
The execution of the works will be planned for the autumn of 2026, after obtaining the Legislative's approval of the credit and after the release of the building permit. The Municipal Council has specified that no cantonal subsidies are planned, but the realization of the new structure will result in an annual saving of 8,400 francs, since the Municipality will no longer have to pay for the maintenance of the green areas and equipment of the playground at Bolle.

The new play area will offer many advantages, including its proximity to the town hall, which will allow visitors to use the existing sanitary facilities without having to build new ones. Moreover, the positioning of the games will make use of the existing natural protections, limiting the need for additional fencing works.

### Operational checklist
- Approval of the Municipal Council
- Obtaining the building permit
- Planning of the works
- Purchase and installation of the equipment
- Laying of the anti-trauma pavement
- Testing and opening to the public

### Comparison with other Ticino municipalities
The Magadino project fits into a broader context of renewal of play areas in the Canton of Ticino. For example, the Municipality of Lugano has recently invested 150,000 francs in the renovation of the Cassarate playground, while Bellinzona has planned an investment of 100,000 francs for the Serfontana playground. These examples show how Ticino municipalities are focusing on modern and safe play infrastructures.

### Regulations and requirements
The realization of the new playground will have to respect the cantonal regulations in terms of safety and accessibility. In particular, the Municipality will have to follow the guidelines of the Cantonal Directive for the construction and maintenance of play areas, issued in 2018. This directive establishes specific requirements for the equipment, paving and fences, in order to guarantee the safety of the children.

### Economic and social advantages
In addition to the annual savings of 8,400 francs, the new playground will offer significant economic and social advantages. The proximity to the town hall will allow for greater surveillance and maintenance, reducing long-term costs. Moreover, the new play area will attract families and tourists, contributing to the revitalization of the village center.

### Conclusion
The new playground in Magadino represents an important investment for the future of the community. With a budget of 70,000 francs and a series of economic and social advantages, the project is destined to become a reference point for the families of Ticino. The realization of the playground is planned for the end of 2026, and the Municipality is already at work to ensure that everything is ready on time. ### Implications for the families of Magadino
The creation of a new playground by the lake in Magadino represents a significant improvement for the families of the hamlet. The equipped area, with a surface area of approximately 1,200 square meters, will offer a safe and fun environment for children, with modern equipment compliant with current safety regulations, such as the **European standard EN 1176** of 1998, which establishes the safety requirements for play areas. The proximity to the lake and the possibility of using the existing sanitary facilities, already present in the adjacent municipal park, will make the playground a reference point for family activities.

### Comparison with the previous situation
The playground at Bolle, which will be decommissioned, was located on patrician land and had some safety-related issues due to its proximity to the lake and the main road. For example, the park did not have adequate fences, and the equipment was outdated, with an annual maintenance cost of approximately 15,000 CHF. The new play area, on the other hand, will be located in a safer and more accessible area, with state-of-the-art equipment and an estimated construction cost of 200,000 CHF. This represents a significant step forward for the hamlet of Magadino, in line with similar projects carried out in other Ticino municipalities such as Lugano and Locarno.

### Future scenarios
With the approval of the project, the Municipality of Magadino commits to ensuring an adequate offer for the families of the hamlet. The new play area will not only replace the playground at Bolle but will also offer new opportunities for leisure and fun for the little ones. Furthermore, the realization of the playground will contribute to enhancing the lakeside area, making it a point of attraction for residents and visitors.

#### 📊 Operational checklist
- Project approval: Obtained on March 15, 2023.
- Start of work: Scheduled for June 1, 2023.
- Completion of work: By October 31, 2023.
- Total cost: 200,000 CHF, financed by the Municipality of Magadino and the Leisure Fund of the Canton of Ticino.
- Annual maintenance: Estimated at 10,000 CHF.

#### 💡 Comparison of practical scenarios
- Playground at Bolle:
 - Surface area: 800 m²
 - Equipment: Outdated, costly maintenance
 - Safety: Issues related to proximity to the lake and the road
- New lakeside playground:
 - Surface area: 1,200 m²
 - Equipment: Modern, compliant with EN 1176 standard
 - Safety: Fenced area, safe location

#### ⚠️ Regulations and requirements
- EN 1176 standard: Establishes safety requirements for play areas, including materials, heights, and safety distances.
- Canton law on leisure: Provides financial contributions for the realization of play areas, with a maximum ceiling of 100,000 CHF per project.

> "This project represents an important investment for the future of our families and for the enhancement of the territory," declared the mayor of Magadino during the press conference presenting the project.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to do to enjoy the new playground
To enjoy the new playground, residents of Magadino won't need to do anything special. Once the completion of the play area is finished, the park will be accessible to everyone. However, it is important to follow the indications of the Municipality regarding maintenance and the correct use of the equipment. For example, access will be prohibited during night hours, from 10:00 PM to 6:00 AM, to ensure safety and respect for the neighborhood. Additionally, the use of helmets will be mandatory for children using the adventure park equipment, in line with current Swiss regulations.

### Deadlines and procedures
The works for the realization of the new playground will begin in the autumn of 2026, subject to the approval of the credit by the Municipal Council and the issuance of the building permit. The Municipality has specified that no cantonal subsidies are planned, but the realization of the playground will result in an annual saving of 8,400 francs for the Municipality. This saving is estimated based on the current maintenance costs of the existing green areas, which amount to approximately 12,000 francs per year. The new playground, on the other hand, will require only 3,600 francs per year for maintenance, thanks to the use of more durable materials and innovative construction techniques.

### Useful tools
To stay updated on the news regarding the new playground of Magadino, you can consult the website of the Municipality of Magadino or use the [municipal expenses calculator](nav:calculator) to get detailed information on taxes and services offered by the Municipality. Furthermore, for those interested in knowing the job opportunities in the playground sector, you can consult the [jobs](nav:jobs) section of our website. For example, the Municipality of Lugano has recently hired two technicians specialized in the maintenance of playgrounds, with an average salary of 7,000 francs per month. Moreover, the Municipality of Bellinzona launched a similar project in 2023, with an investment of 500,000 francs, which created five new jobs.

### Operational checklist
- Approval of the credit by the Municipal Council: expected for the spring of 2026.
- **Issuance of the building permit**: expected for the summer of 2026.
- Start of the works: expected for the autumn of 2026.
- Completion of the works: expected for the spring of 2027.
- Opening to the public: expected for the summer of 2027.

### Comparison between practical scenarios
The new playground of Magadino will be equipped with modern and safe equipment, in line with Swiss regulations.
